title: `PyThreadState_GetFrame()` thread-safety is unclear (and inconsistent with `sys._current_frames()`)
author: devdanzin

comments.author: colesbury
comments.body:
I think the intent is option (a) -- it should be the current thread.

I think when the docs say "attached" or "must be attached" it always means for the current thread: 

https://docs.python.org/3/glossary.html#term-attached-thread-state:~:text=A%20thread%20state%20that%20is%20active%20for%20the%20current%20OS%20thread.
